title: Path Sum
description: Given a binary tree and a targetSum, determine if the tree has a root-to-leaf path such that adding up all the values along the path equals targetSum. A leaf is a node with no children.

examples.explanation: There are no root-to-leaf paths that sum to 5.
An empty tree has no paths, hence cannot sum to any value.

constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 5000].
Each node's value is in the range [-1000, 1000].
hints: Consider using recursion to explore each path from the root to the leaves.
Keep track of the current sum as you traverse the tree.
Check if a node is a leaf before deciding if the current sum equals targetSum.
You can also use an iterative approach with a stack for depth-first traversal.
